feelings.title: 🎵 How Music Makes Us Feel
feelings.html:
Music gives us many feelings. When we listen to fast music, we feel excited. We want to dance and move! When we listen to slow, soft music, we feel calm and sometimes sleepy.

Some music makes us feel nervous. Scary music in films makes our hearts beat fast! But beautiful, quiet music gives us peace. We feel peaceful and relaxed.

Happy music makes us smile. Sad music can make us cry. When we feel sad, slow music can help us feel better. When we feel unhappy, listening to our favourite song always helps!

Music can also make us feel proud. When we hear our country's song, we feel proud and strong. Music is special because it changes how we feel. It can make us feel well when we are not feeling good.

We listen to music live at concerts. We also listen to music at home, in the car, or at school. There is always a light, happy song to make our day better!

concert.title: 🎶 The School Concert
concert.html:
It was Friday afternoon. The school concert was at four o'clock. All the children were excited! Emma was nervous because she was singing in the choir.

'When does the concert start?' asked Tom. 'It starts at four o'clock on Friday,' said the teacher. 'The next concert is in March.'

First, the choir sang a happy song. The audience started to smile. Then, Lily played the piano. The music was soft and peaceful. Some parents felt sleepy because it was so calm!

Next, Sam played the drums. The music was fast and loud! Everyone felt excited. The light on the stage was bright and colourful.

At the end, all the children sang together. The parents felt very proud. Some parents were so happy that they started to cry! Emma did not feel nervous anymore. She felt well and happy.

'How often do you have concerts?' asked a parent. 'We have concerts in December and in June,' said the teacher. It was a wonderful live concert!
